summary: preinstall script exfiltrates host/user info to hardcoded external host
details: The package ships a lifecycle script at dist/preinstall.js that executes on `npm install`. The script pulls in `os`, `fs`, `https`, and `http`, gathers host identifiers via `os.userInfo()` and `os.networkInterfaces()`, reads `process.env`, and issues outbound `https.get(...)` calls to the hardcoded host `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name` (L71, L73, L103, L104). The destination is not a publisher-owned CDN, is not a known runtime/SDK host, and the purpose of the fetch is not documented by the package. The combination of (a) install-time lifecycle trigger, (b) collection of user/network/environment identifiers, and (c) transmission to an undocumented third-party host matches the classic install-time reconnaissance/exfiltration pattern. Any developer or CI runner installing this package leaks host fingerprint data to the operator of `2tak.l.serverhost.name` without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
